editableNewDialogue.text: do you think robots will ever write movies better than humans?

They might get very good at certain parts—plot structure, pacing, even dialogue in a specific style—because they can learn patterns from lots of scripts. But “better” is subjective: humans bring lived experience, cultural context, and intentional themes. A likely near-term outcome is collaboration: humans set vision and meaning, tools help draft scenes, brainstorm twists, or polish language.
what about jobs, will robots take everything?

More likely: tasks get automated, not entire jobs. Repetitive, predictable work tends to be affected first; roles that involve complex judgment, care, negotiation, or hands-on work in messy environments are harder to automate. Historically, technology removes some tasks while creating new ones (operators, technicians, designers, auditors, safety roles), but transitions can still be disruptive without training and policy support.
give me an example of robots helping in daily life

A practical example is a hospital using delivery robots to move linens, medications, or lab samples between floors, freeing staff for patient-facing work. Another is warehouse robots that bring shelves to human pickers to reduce walking time and speed up orders.

One neat example: NASA’s Mars helicopter Ingenuity flew in an atmosphere that’s about 1% as dense as Earth’s at sea level, so it needed very fast-spinning rotors and lightweight design to generate enough lift. It helped show aerial scouting is possible on another planet.
i still practicing that python stuff at nights after work, but the loops does confuse me sometimes

That’s normal—loops click after a bit of repetition. Tell me which one is tripping you up more (for-loops or while-loops) and what you’re trying to build, and I’ll walk you through it with a small example.
hey i gotta correct something i said before
Sure—what would you like to correct?
when i told you i like to play keyboard and guitars, that was not true. i dont play any instruments.
Thanks for clarifying. So to confirm: you don’t play keyboard or guitar (or any instruments), and your earlier message was incorrect.
